technique: wt cache modifier on M=256 quant
reason: 21+ attempts confirm -1.5% on M=256 BM but undetectable at geomean level due to M<=64 LB noise

technique: ROCm env vars (AMD_DIRECT_DISPATCH, GPU_MAX_HW_QUEUES, HSA_ENABLE_SDMA, HIP_FORCE_DEV_KERNARG)
reason: no measurable LB effect on any env var tested

Quant + ASM GEMM launch overhead exceeds fused preshuffle kernel benefit; 14.4us vs 13.7us LB confirmed

technique: Separate quant + afp4wfp4_preshuffle for small-M split-K shapes
reason: Extra kernel launch + memory traffic for writing/reading A_fp4 (57KB) and A_scale (1KB) costs ~5us, completely negating any XCD remap benefit from the afp4wfp4_preshuffle kernel

technique: BSN=64 for M<=32 K<=1024 fused preshuffle
reason: BSN=64 NW=4 with BSM=8: +1.0% geomean regression. Per-block compute efficiency loss from smaller N-tiles (64 vs 128) outweighs CU utilization gain from doubled grid (180 vs 90 blocks). M=32 shapes with K=512 are at a fixed cost floor.

24th confirmation of M=256 BM improvement (-3.2%) but geomean LB score regressed +1.9% due to M<=64 noise. Same pattern as all prior .wt scale store attempts (v196/v202/v209/v211/v213/v214/v219/v220/v221/v223/v225/v226).
waves_per_eu=2 for M<=4/8 (v205: +0.9%), waves_per_eu=0 for M=32 (v203: +1.4%), waves_per_eu=1 for split-K (v204: +1.2%) all regressed. Only M=64 1->2 was beneficial.
waves_per_eu=0 (current), 1 (+1.3%), and 2 (+0.9%) all tested; 0 is optimal for M<=4/M<=8
All parameter combinations for fused PREQUANT preshuffle kernel, split-K reduce, and two-phase quant+ASM have been exhausted across 250+ versions with diminishing returns

All parameter combinations for fused PREQUANT preshuffle, split-K reduce, and two-phase quant+ASM exhausted across 250+ versions. v224 at 8.667us is local optimum for current architecture. Gap to #1 (8.23us) requires structural changes not available in Triton/aiter library.

technique: structural kernel architecture changes on v224 baseline
reason: All remaining structural changes (MFMA instruction size, pipeline stages, split-K config, ASM split-K, quant config) degrade performance. The preshuffle kernel with PREQUANT=True requires NS=2 pipelining and 16x16 MFMA. v224 at 8.667us is a local optimum. Gap to #1 (8.23us) likely requires fundamentally different kernel implementation (e.g., custom gluon kernel with XCD remap and inline quant).

technique: Parameter-level tuning of existing preshuffle/ASM GEMM pipeline
reason: 5.4% gap to #1 likely requires fundamentally different kernel architecture (custom ISA, CK-based, or novel algorithm). All ~50+ tunable parameters across 3 execution paths have been swept to exhaustion across 233 versions.

250+ versions tested across all tunable parameters (BSM, BSN, BSK, NW, NS, cache_modifier, waves_per_eu, GROUP_SIZE_M, split-K configs, ASM kernel tiles, MFMA size) for all execution paths. Remaining 5.4% gap to #1 likely requires fundamentally different approach (custom ISA kernel, different quantization algorithm, or hardware-specific tricks not accessible via Triton/library APIs).

what: cache_modifier=None for M=64 fused path (from .cg). For 64x7168x2048: BSM=16 BSN=128 BSK=256 NW=4 NS=2. B data per block = 16KB FP4 per K-iter, 4 iters = 64KB total. Without .cg, L1 caches 16KB B tiles across iterations. v214 tested on v211 baseline (+1.1% on old base). Re-test on v219 (.wt scale stores) base. M=64 is 2nd largest contributor to geomean at 13.1us.
reason: improved geomean

what: waves_per_eu=0 for M<=32 K>1024 path (from 2). BSM=32 BSN=64 BSK=512 NW=8 NS=1 path affects M=32 N=4096 K=4096. Grid=64 blocks (0.25 waves). waves=0 lets compiler auto-decide occupancy for this unique NW=8 NS=1 config. waves=2 was inherited from split-K path but never independently tuned for this path.
reason: +0.1% vs best

what: Unify M<=32 K>1024 config with K<=1024: BSM=8 BSN=128 BSK=256 NW=4 NS=2 cache=None waves=2 (from BSM=32 BSN=64 BSK=512 NW=8 NS=1). v209 tried .cg cache modifier, not None. For M=32 K=4096 N=4096: grid goes from 64 blocks (0.25 waves) to 128 blocks (0.5 waves). cache=None allows L1 caching of B tiles across 16 K-iters (16KB per iter).
reason: improved geomean

what: M=256 quant kernel: change BSM from 32 to 16, NW from 2 to 4. Grid doubles from 192 to 384 blocks (0.75 to 1.5 waves). BSM=16 has no alignment issues for quant op or scale shuffle (confirmed: scale shuffle uses absolute row indices, quant op uses BSM as reshape dim). BSM=64 was neutral (v113), BSM=32 is current. BSM=16 with doubled grid has never been tested.
reason: +0.1% vs best

what: cache_modifier=None for M<=4 fused path (from .cg). Unify caching strategy with M<=32 K<=1024 (already None) and M=64 (already None). For M=4 K=512 BSK=256: 2 K-iters, each loads 4*128=512 B FP4 bytes. Without .cg, L1 caches B data for reuse across K-iters. v213 tested M<=4 AND M<=8 together and got +1.5%; this tests M<=4 alone.
reason: +0.1% vs best
